person.name: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen

nameForms.fullText: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen

sibling.name: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen

zh.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen是Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen的兒子，於1045年出生。他和Adeline de Bocholt至少有1個女兒。他去世於1107年享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen是Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen的儿子，于1045年出生。他和Adeline de Bocholt至少有1个女儿。他去世于1107年享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen was born in 1045 as the son of Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. He had at least 1 daughter with Adeline de Bocholt. He died in 1107, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen est né en 1045, fils de Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Adeline de Bocholt. Il est décédé en 1107, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen wurde 1045, als Sohn von Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen geboren. Er hatte mit Adeline de Bocholt mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1107 im Alter von 62 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen è nato il nel 1045 come figlio di Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Adeline de Bocholt. Morì nel 1107, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergenは1045年にGeerard van Mechelen van Grimbergenの息子として生まれた。彼はAdeline de Bocholtとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1107年に62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen(은)는 1045년 Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Adeline de Bocholt(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1107년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen nasceu em 1045 filho de Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Adeline de Bocholt. Ele faleceu em 1107, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen родился в 1045 г. Его отца звали Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Adeline de Bocholt. Он умер в 1107 г. в возрасте 62 лет.

sm.from 1090 to 1105: mai le 1090 e o'o I le 1105

es.lifeSummary: Walter I van Mechelen van Grimbergen nació en 1045 como hijo de Geerard van Mechelen van Grimbergen. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Adeline de Bocholt. Murió en 1107, a la edad de 62 años.
